content: Self-Refine’s single-perspective iterative self-feedback framework can be augmented by drawing on LLM self-reflection mechanisms’ focus on multiple inconsistent solving perspectives to create a hybrid multi-cycle self-refinement system: outer cycles use cross-perspective inconsistency to surface unrecognized flaws in the initial output, while inner cycles use Self-Refine’s training-free single-model iteration to resolve those flaws incrementally. This hybrid system retains Self-Refine’s key advantage of requiring no additional training or supervision, while addressing its common limitation of getting stuck in local blind spots caused by uniform self-feedback from a single model perspective.
